mission.title: Install and Configure Runme Notebooks on Kubernetes
mission.description: Runme is a tool that allows you to execute code snippets directly from your markdown files, making it easier to follow operational documentation and runbooks. Installing Runme enables you to create runnable instructions in your documentation, enhancing reliability and usability.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for Runme by running the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace runme
```

steps.title: Apply the Runme Deployment
steps.description:
Deploy Runme by applying the deployment manifest from the GitHub repository:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.9.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-deployment.yaml -n runme
```

steps.title: Apply the Runme Service
steps.description:
Expose the Runme application by applying the service manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.9.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-service.yaml -n runme
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Runme pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n runme
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Runme will show the Runme pods in a running state when you check with `kubectl get pods`. You will also be able to access the Runme service locally via port forwarding.

uninstall.title: Remove Runme Resources
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Runme, delete the deployment and service using the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.9.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-deployment.yaml -n runme
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.9.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-service.yaml -n runme
kubectl delete namespace runme
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Runme
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Runme, apply the latest deployment and service manifests:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.10.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-deployment.yaml -n runme
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/runmedev/runme/v0.10.0/deploy/kubernetes/runme-service.yaml -n runme
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Runme pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n runme
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Runme service, ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the service endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n runme
kubectl describe svc runme -n runme
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource-related issues, verify that your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated for the Runme deployment.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
